alert.title: High p95 latency and Redis timeout on payment-api
alert.description: p95 latency and error rate exceeded threshold for /checkout

runbooks.title: Redis timeout triage
runbooks.excerpt: Check Redis latency, connection limits, pool configuration, and deploy correlation before rollback.
steps: Confirm p95 latency increase is isolated to checkout/payment paths before paging unrelated services.
Compare Redis timeout count with app connection pool saturation and slow command latency.
Check whether the latest deploy changed pool size, timeout, retry, or circuit-breaker settings.
Verify Redis node health and client-side error budget before considering rollback.
validation_signals: http_latency_p95_ms remains above 1000ms for two consecutive windows
redis_timeout_count is non-zero during the incident window
application logs contain Redis or database timeout messages
recent deploy overlaps the incident start time
safe_actions: Open an owner review with evidence links and current confidence.
Ask the service owner to confirm pool saturation before changing config.
Use the approved rollback reference only after human approval.
avoid: Do not restart pods as a first action unless readiness failures or crash loops are confirmed.
Do not increase connection limits without checking downstream Redis capacity.

runbooks.title: Payment dependency saturation checklist
runbooks.excerpt: Use when latency evidence points to connection pools, Redis, or database saturation without confirmed service outage.
steps: Check client pool usage, active connections, timeout rate, and retry rate.
Compare dependency latency with service CPU and memory to avoid misattributing local saturation.
Review trace bottleneck spans for the affected endpoint.
Attach findings to the CDO Slack/Jira handoff event.
validation_signals: dependency latency contributes the longest trace span
retry count increases after the latest deploy
safe_actions: Keep recommendation as human review until pool and dependency evidence agree.
Prefer rollback consideration over ad hoc config changes when deploy correlation is strong.
avoid: Do not assign root cause to Redis if traces show app CPU saturation instead.
